message:
chore: sync final multi-agent changes

Captured remaining changes from parallel agent sessions:
- beads: issue status updates
- chrome: demo showcase chrome updates

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -764,8 +764,8 @@
 {"id":"bd-l8x9.2.5","title":"Backdrop Composition: Stack Multiple FX Layers With One Buffer Apply","description":"Add a library-grade way to stack multiple BackdropFx layers (e.g., gradient + metaballs + noise) while preserving performance: compute/composite into a single bg buffer and apply to the frame once.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Stacking multiple effects requires only a single pass of `buf.set` over cells (one final apply to the frame).
- [ ] No per-frame allocations in steady state (buffers reused).
- [ ] Layer ordering semantics are explicit and deterministic (A over B vs B over A is documented and tested).
- [ ] Unit tests cover 2-layer composition equivalence and no-alloc steady-state behavior.

## Extreme Optimization
- [ ] Composition uses tight loops and avoids redundant per-layer per-cell work; measure before/after with representative scenes.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-02T23:15:59.585083254Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T04:03:37.988257433Z","closed_at":"2026-02-03T04:03:37.988237225Z","close_reason":"Implemented StackedFx compositor for multi-layer BackdropFx composition with single-pass output. Features: BlendMode enum (Over/Additive/Multiply/Screen), FxLayer wrapper, grow-only buffer reuse, 14 unit tests covering composition, blending, and no-alloc behavior.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["extras","performance","visual"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-l8x9.2.5","depends_on_id":"bd-l8x9.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-02T23:15:59.585083254Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":221,"issue_id":"bd-l8x9.2.5","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## User Value

"Trivially composable" should not mean "N full-screen passes per layer".

Users should be able to do:
- subtle gradient base
- metaballs at 0.20 opacity
- plasma at 0.10 opacity
- scrim under text

…without paying O(layers * w*h) buffer writes to the frame.

## Proposed API Shapes (pick one)

A) `BackdropStack` widget
- owns:
  - `Vec<PackedRgba>` main buffer
  - `Vec<PackedRgba>` scratch buffer
- renders:
  - fill base
  - for each layer:
    - effect renders into scratch
    - apply layer opacity -> composite scratch over main
  - apply main to frame once

B) `CompositeFx` implementing `BackdropFx`
- wraps multiple `BackdropFx` and composes them into a caller-provided dst
- still needs scratch (owned by wrapper) but integrates cleanly with existing `Backdrop` widget

C) Tuple impls (advanced)
- implement `BackdropFx` for `(A, B)` / `(A, B, C)` to avoid trait objects
- may still need scratch; keep it explicit

## Performance Rules

- No per-frame allocations (buffers reused).
- No `Box<dyn BackdropFx>` unless we can prove it doesn’t matter (prefer generics).
- Composition must be stable/deterministic.

## Tests To Add

- unit: 2-layer composition equals sequential rendering + Buffer::set semantics
- unit: no-alloc steady-state
- unit: ordering matters and is documented (A over B vs B over A)

## Acceptance

- [ ] stacking multiple effects requires only a single pass of `buf.set` over cells
- [ ] API is ergonomic enough to use in `bd-l8x9.7` (markdown-over-backdrop)
- [ ] layering is deterministic and well-documented","created_at":"2026-02-02T23:16:11Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-l8x9.3","title":"Extract Metaballs Into ftui-extras (BackdropFx)","description":"## Goal
Extract metaballs into `ftui-extras` as a reusable `BackdropFx` implementation.

## Scope
- Move core field math + colorization into `crates/ftui-extras/src/visual_fx/effects/metaballs.rs`.
- Provide `MetaballsParams` + a small set of presets that derive colors from `ThemeInputs`.
- Implement quality scaling (`FxQuality`) so reduced budgets stay responsive.

## Tests / Validation
- Unit tests: determinism under fixed inputs; tiny-area safety.
- No-alloc proxy: repeated renders at stable size do not grow internal buffers.
- Integration: used as a backdrop behind a text panel (validated via bd-l8x9.7).

## Acceptance Criteria
- Metaballs is usable as a library effect (not demo-owned code).
- No per-frame allocations required by the effect.","notes":"MetaballsFx module files added under crates/ftui-extras/src/visual_fx/effects/ (no visual_fx.rs wiring yet). Waiting on visual_fx.rs reservations (BronzeGrove/CloudyCove) to export + integrate tests.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:33:51.868818039Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T04:05:48.131412899Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["extras","visual"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-l8x9.3","depends_on_id":"bd-l8x9","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:33:51.868818039Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-l8x9.3","depends_on_id":"bd-l8x9.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:33:58.547450791Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":176,"issue_id":"bd-l8x9.3","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Context

Metaballs are currently implemented in:
- `crates/ftui-demo-showcase/src/screens/visual_effects.rs` (`MetaballsState`)

They compute an implicit field sum and colorize by weighted hue.

## Goal

Provide a library-grade `MetaballsFx` that can be used as:
- a cell-background backdrop behind real widgets
- (optionally later) a canvas/high-res demo renderer

## Design Requirements

- deterministic output given `(w, h, t, params)`
- no per-frame allocations
- quality scaling: fewer balls / fewer iterations / lower frequency at Reduced/Minimal
- theme-aware palette: do not hardcode demo palettes; use ThemeInputs

## Implementation Notes

- Port the state and update logic, but expose:
  - `MetaballsParams` / presets (e.g., "Aurora", "Lava", "Ocean")
  - `MetaballsFx::step(dt)` or accept absolute time `t`

- Rendering target: fill `out[y*w+x] = PackedRgba` backgrounds.

- Performance: avoid per-pixel sqrt; keep dist^2 math; consider precomputing normalized coords per column/row when beneficial.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Metaballs moved out of demo screen into `ftui-extras`.
- [ ] Backdrop composition works (metaballs behind text panel).
- [ ] Presets exist and look good under at least 2 themes.
- [ ] Reduced/Minimal modes produce a cheaper but still attractive output.

## Tests

- determinism test: fixed seed/params -> stable output hash
- bounds safety: tiny w/h does not panic
- perf smoke: rendering does not allocate in steady state
","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:34:10Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-l8x9.3.1","title":"MetaballsFx: Port Core Field + Colorization (Cell-Space)","description":"## Task
Port the core metaballs field computation to cell-space rendering with explicit sampling and colorization hooks.

## Scope
- Field equation + thresholding for blobs.
- Distance/smoothing parameters normalized by `FxQuality`.
- Output colorization separate from field math (so palettes can be swapped).

## Tests / Validation
- Unit tests: field continuity, threshold boundaries.
- Determinism test: fixed seed + time yields identical cell values.
- Tiny-area test: 1x1/2x2 buffer does not panic and is stable.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Field math produces stable shapes across time.
- No division-by-zero or NaN under degenerate sizes.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:34:19.392928744Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T01:18:23.354728219Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["visual"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-l8x9.3.1","depends_on_id":"bd-l8x9.1.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-02T23:14:57.405890558Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-l8x9.3.1","depends_on_id":"bd-l8x9.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:34:19.392928744Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":177,"issue_id":"bd-l8x9.3.1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Notes:
- Source reference: `MetaballsState::render()` in `visual_effects.rs`.
- Replace `(px,py)` loop over painter space with `(x,y)` loop over cells.
- Keep threshold/glow thresholds but parameterize them.
- Output color should include alpha (optionally <255) so caller can tune opacity via composition.

Acceptance:
- [ ] No allocations during render (no Vec push, no per-pixel Vec).
- [ ] Output uses ThemeInputs-driven hues/accents instead of hardcoded HSV palettes.
","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:34:25Z"},{"id":239,"issue_id":"bd-l8x9.3.1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Started. visual_fx.rs still reserved by BronzeGrove (bd-l8x9.2.2); waiting for release before code changes. Will port MetaballsState::render into BackdropFx cell-space with palette hooks and tests.","created_at":"2026-02-03T01:18:23Z"}]}
-{"id":"bd-l8x9.3.2","title":"MetaballsFx: Params + Presets (Theme-Aware)","description":"## Task
Define `MetaballsParams` + presets that are deterministic, theme-aware, and quality-scaled.

## Scope
- Parameters: blob count, radius range, speed, smoothing factor, glow/threshold behavior.
- Presets (initial): Subtle, Vivid, HighContrast.
- Theme integration: derive palette choices from `ThemeInputs` (accents + bg).

## Tests / Validation
- Unit tests: preset values stay within safe bounds (no NaN, no division-by-zero scenarios).
- Snapshot/regression tests: presets are visually distinct and remain legible under a foreground overlay (scrim).

## Acceptance Criteria
- Presets are deterministic and theme-aware.
- Default preset produces a readable backdrop without manual tuning.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:34:32.889515487Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-02T23:33:29.017462610Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["theme","visual"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-l8x9.3.2","depends_on_id":"bd-l8x9.1.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:41:58.064209943Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-l8x9.3.2","depends_on_id":"bd-l8x9.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:34:32.889515487Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":178,"issue_id":"bd-l8x9.3.2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Params (suggested)

- ball_count (or implicit via Vec<Ball>)
- radius_range
- velocity_range
- field_threshold / glow_threshold
- color_mode:
  - AccentMix (mix between theme accent slots)
  - HueWheel (derive hues from time)

## Presets (examples)

- Aurora: cool accents, soft glow
- Lava: warm accents, higher contrast
- Ocean: cyan/blue accents, smoother motion

## Quality Scaling

- Full: 6-8 balls
- Reduced: 4-5 balls
- Minimal: 2-3 balls + cheaper color

Acceptance:
- [ ] Metaballs looks good without demo-specific palette helpers.
- [ ] Presets are stable across themes.
","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:34:39Z"}]}
-{"id":"bd-l8x9.3.3","title":"MetaballsFx: Determinism + Tiny-Area + No-Alloc Tests","description":"## Task
Add determinism, tiny-area, and no-allocation regression tests for MetaballsFx.

## Tests
- Determinism: fixed inputs (dims/time/params/theme/quality) produce a stable output hash.
- Tiny-area safety: 0x0/1x1/2x2 buffers do not panic.
- No-alloc proxy: repeated renders at stable size do not grow any internal buffers/capacities.
- Quality behavior: Reduced/Minimal produce output (and/or skip) as documented.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Tests cover edge sizes, determinism, and allocation behavior.
- Failures are debuggable (print the parameters that produced the failing hash).","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:34:44.586858050Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-02T23:34:00.442191217Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["tests"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-l8x9.3.3","depends_on_id":"bd-l8x9.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:34:44.586858050Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":179,"issue_id":"bd-l8x9.3.3","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Test ideas:
- deterministic hash: render_bg on fixed params/time and hash the resulting PackedRgba buffer
- tiny area safety: w/h in {1,2} and narrow shapes
- steady-state no-alloc: call render_bg repeatedly with same buffer and assert capacity unchanged (plus optional allocator instrumentation later)

Acceptance:
- [ ] tests live alongside the fx module (unit tests)
- [ ] tests do not depend on demo showcase code
","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:34:50Z"}]}

## Presets (examples)

+{"id":"bd-l8x9.3.3","title":"MetaballsFx: Determinism + Tiny-Area + No-Alloc Tests","description":"## Task
Add determinism, tiny-area, and no-allocation regression tests for MetaballsFx.

## Tests
- Determinism: fixed inputs (dims/time/params/theme/quality) produce a stable output hash.
- Tiny-area safety: 0x0/1x1/2x2 buffers do not panic.
- No-alloc proxy: repeated renders at stable size do not grow any internal buffers/capacities.
- Quality behavior: Reduced/Minimal produce output (and/or skip) as documented.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Tests cover edge sizes, determinism, and allocation behavior.
- Failures are debuggable (print the parameters that produced the failing hash).","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:34:44.586858050Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T04:15:46.611133561Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["tests"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-l8x9.3.3","depends_on_id":"bd-l8x9.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:34:44.586858050Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":179,"issue_id":"bd-l8x9.3.3","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Test ideas:

- steady-state no-alloc: call render_bg repeatedly with same buffer and assert capacity unchanged (plus optional allocator instrumentation later)

Acceptance:
- [ ] tests live alongside the fx module (unit tests)
- [ ] tests do not depend on demo showcase code

 {"id":"bd-l8x9.4","title":"Extract Plasma Into ftui-extras (BackdropFx)","description":"## Goal
Extract plasma into `ftui-extras` as a reusable `BackdropFx` implementation.

## Scope
- Port the wave interference function into `crates/ftui-extras/src/visual_fx/effects/plasma.rs`.
- Provide theme-aware palettes/presets derived from `ThemeInputs`.
- Implement quality scaling (`FxQuality`) to reduce trig work at lower budgets.

## Tests / Validation
- Unit tests: output bounds, determinism, tiny-area safety.
- No-alloc proxy: repeated renders at stable size do not grow buffers.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Plasma is usable as a library effect (not demo-owned code).
- No per-frame allocations required by the effect.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:34:54.473044709Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T04:05:53.860620349Z","closed_at":"2026-02-03T04:05:53.860600151Z","close_reason":"PlasmaFx extracted to ftui-extras/src/visual_fx/effects/plasma.rs with full BackdropFx implementation, theme-aware palettes, quality scaling, and tests","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["extras","visual"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-l8x9.4","depends_on_id":"bd-l8x9","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:34:54.473044709Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-l8x9.4","depends_on_id":"bd-l8x9.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:34:59.223899474Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-l8x9.4","depends_on_id":"bd-l8x9.3","type":"related","created_at":"2026-02-02T23:02:47.193004313Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":180,"issue_id":"bd-l8x9.4","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Context

Plasma is currently implemented in:
- `crates/ftui-demo-showcase/src/screens/visual_effects.rs` (`PlasmaState`)

It is a multi-wave interference pattern with palette switching.

## Goal

Provide a library-grade `PlasmaFx` as a BackdropFx:
- deterministic
- fast, no allocations
- theme-aware palettes
- quality scaling to keep runtime responsive

## Key Design Choices

- Plasma does not need internal mutable state beyond time/palette; it can be pure `t -> color(x,y)`.
- Prefer a small number of theme-derived palettes rather than demo-only hardcoded palettes.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Plasma moved into `ftui-extras`.
- [ ] Supports palette variants (theme-derived) without requiring demo helpers.
- [ ] Reduced/Minimal modes are cheaper (fewer sine calls / simplified function).
- [ ] Works as a backdrop behind a text widget without harming legibility.
","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:35:08Z"},{"id":214,"issue_id":"bd-l8x9.4","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Dependency Tweak (Parallelism)

Plasma extraction does not strictly depend on metaballs extraction once the shared API (`bd-l8x9.1` + `bd-l8x9.2`) is defined.

Action:
- Changed `bd-l8x9.4 -> bd-l8x9.3` from `blocks` to `related`.

Rationale:
- Allows metaballs and plasma to be implemented in parallel after the API/widget exist.
- Preserves the intent that both should share the same composability/perf conventions.","created_at":"2026-02-02T23:02:52Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-l8x9.4.1","title":"PlasmaFx: Port Core Wave Function (Cell-Space)","description":"## Task
Port the core plasma wave function to cell-space rendering with explicit sampling steps.

## Scope
- Base wave equation (sum of sines) with normalized coordinates.
- Quality-controlled sampling step and palette lookup.
- Separate math from color mapping.

## Tests / Validation
- Unit tests: wave output bounds and repeatability.
- Determinism test: fixed seed/time yields identical output.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Wave math stable for all buffer sizes.
- No NaNs or out-of-range color values.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:35:13.593473623Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T01:22:14.960136049Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["visual"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-l8x9.4.1","depends_on_id":"bd-l8x9.1.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-02T23:14:57.606446439Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-l8x9.4.1","depends_on_id":"bd-l8x9.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:35:13.593473623Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":181,"issue_id":"bd-l8x9.4.1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Notes:
- Source reference: `PlasmaState::render()` in `visual_effects.rs`.
- Full mode currently uses ~6 trig evaluations per point.
- Reduced/Minimal modes should cut trig calls (e.g., 3 waves) and/or use cheaper approximations.

Acceptance:
- [ ] No per-frame allocations.
- [ ] Deterministic output given inputs.
","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:35:19Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-l8x9.4.2","title":"PlasmaFx: Theme-Derived Palettes + Presets","description":"## Task
Provide theme-derived palettes and plasma presets tuned for legibility.

## Scope
- Palette generation from `ThemeInputs` with documented fallbacks.
- Presets (initial): Subtle, Neon, Monochrome.
- Quality scaling: Reduced/Minimal should cut trig work and/or simplify palette mapping without breaking the overall look.

## Tests / Validation
- Unit tests: palette stop counts and bounds; deterministic output.
- Snapshot/regression tests: presets produce distinct visuals with readable overlay text when paired with a scrim.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Palettes are deterministic and theme-aware.
- Default preset is safe across light/dark themes.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:35:24.817434914Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-02T23:33:35.773481975Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["theme"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-l8x9.4.2","depends_on_id":"bd-l8x9.1.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:41:58.234835733Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-l8x9.4.2","depends_on_id":"bd-l8x9.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:35:24.817434914Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":182,"issue_id":"bd-l8x9.4.2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Palette strategy:
- Use 2-4 accent colors from ThemeInputs.
- Provide interpolation function (lerp) and multi-stop sampling.
- Keep it local to visual-fx; do not depend on text-effects APIs.

Presets:
- AuroraPlasma (cool)
- EmberPlasma (warm)
- NeonPlasma (high saturation)

Acceptance:
- [ ] Palettes look good across themes.
- [ ] Palette switching does not require allocation.
","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:35:31Z"}]}
